question: [File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: toOpenApiPath()] Can an unprivileged external user send thousands of requests to arbitrary unregistered paths (where req.route is null) so that req.path is used verbatim as the path label, causing unbounded label cardinality in requestTotalCounter, durationHistogram, requestSizeHistogram, and responseSizeHistogram, exhausting the Node.js process heap and degrading service for all users?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a crafted content-length header set to Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER (9007199254740991) so that requestSizeHistogram.record() records astronomically large values, permanently corrupting the api_request_size_bytes histogram and making it useless for capacity planning?
